response.justification: While this candidate is clearly a talented senior engineer with strong software fundamentals and excellent communication skills, they fundamentally misalign with the role's core technical requirements. They have only 6 months of documented C++ experience (from 2014–2015), whereas the position requires 5–10 years on Windows platforms. They lack any SQL Server or Oracle expertise, and critically, have zero capital markets or fixed income domain experience. Their career trajectory over the past decade has been web and systems development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), moving away from C++ and finance entirely.
strengths: Excellent communication and mentorship—organized workshops, gave conference talks, maintains multiple open-source projects with strong documentation
Strong software engineering practices including TDD, CI/CD pipeline experience, and trunk-based development across multiple roles
Senior-level architectural thinking and polyglot programming ability demonstrated through staff roles at Google and Meta
concerns: Critical gap: only 6 months of documented C++ experience (2014–2015), with no Windows platform development mentioned; role requires 5–10 years of hands-on C++
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; resume lists basic SQL and MySQL only, far below the mandatory 'strong' requirement for capital markets systems

Completely absent financial services domain expertise—career spans geospatial, media automation, and web platforms, with zero exposure to fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking

key_factors.factor: C++ experience gap (5–10 years required vs. 6 months documented, 10+ years stale)
No capital markets or fixed income domain expertise (mandatory requirement or strong alternative)
